rulings.Case Title: SUSAN HULL BOSTWICK VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 24, 2019, Line 2, RESPONDENT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, ANDREW AMARA DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION Respondents Franchise Tax Board and Andrew Amara's demurrer to Petitioner Susan Hull Bostwick's first amended petition for writ of mandamus is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Petitioner's assertion that there is no tax law in effect is not a factual allegation, but a legal argument that is not entitled to deference on a demurrer. (See McBride v. Boughton (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 379, 384 ["We treat the demurrer as admitting all material facts properly pleaded, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law."].) Revenue and Taxation Code sections 17001 et seq. and 19001 et seq. provide for payment of personal income tax to the Franchise Tax Board, so Petitioner is challenging tax assessments provided for by statute, not criminal activity such as extortion, robbery, and racketeering. Article XIII, section 32 of the California Constitution "explicitly bars a proceeding against the State for issuance of a writ of administrative mandamus when the purpose of the proceeding is to prevent or enjoin the collection of 'any tax.'" (First Aid Services of San Diego, Inc. v. California Employment Development Department (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1470, 1480.) Furthermore, Petitioner fails to plead that she has paid any of the challenged tax assessments outside of the 2014 tax year or that she has exhausted her administrative remedies as required by Revenue and Taxation Code sections 19381, 19382, and 19385. (See Loeffler v. Target Corp. (2014) 58 Cal.4th 1081, 1107-08 [explaining that under a parallel sales tax statute a taxpayer must first pay the tax and give the Board an opportunity to correct mistakes through a refund before the taxpayer may bring suit].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Respondents is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JEAN W. SETO ET AL VS. KWOK HUNG SZETO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery For The Limited Purpose Of Taking The Deposition Of Charles Thompson
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 24, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, TINA CHIN, ALEXANDER WONG, and ERIC WONG's Motion To Reopen Discovery For The Limited Purpose Of Taking The Deposition Of Charles Thompson Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Discovery is reopened for the sole purpose of taking the deposition of Charles Thompson. There is no prejudice to Defendants in allowing this deposition to go forward. The parties stipulation with regard to expert discovery remains in effect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN W. SETO ET AL VS. KWOK HUNG SZETO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 24, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFFs JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, JEAN SETO, TINA CHIN, ALEXANDER WONG, ERIC WONG's Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint Plaintiffs Jean W. Seto, HM Wong Group, Tina Wong Chin, Alexander Hans Wong, and Eric Kim Wong's motion for leave to file fourth amended complaint is GRANTED. California has a policy of liberally permitting amendments to pleadings at any stage of the proceeding. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 760.) Similarly, Code of Civil Procedure section 474 liberally allows Doe amendments where Plaintiffs were ignorant of facts that would support a cause of action against the new Defendant, even if Plaintiffs knew the identity of the Defendant. (McNair v. Worldwide Church of God (1987) 197 Cal.App.3d 363, 379.) Through depositions of Defendants Kwok Hung Szeto, Nikki Szeto, and Patrick Szeto, Plaintiffs learned of new facts that potentially implicate Charles M. Thompson and his law firm in the dispute alleged in the complaint. Defendants have not shown that they will be prejudiced by the Court allowing the filing of the fourth amended complaint because trial is three months away, the new allegations arise from the same core facts, and any new discovery will be limited to the involvement of the lawyers in the alleged misconduct. However, absent a ruling granting Plaintiffs' motion for leave to reopen discovery, this order does not entitle Plaintiffs to seek any discovery related to the new allegations or the new cause of action in the fourth amended complaint. The Court declines to rule on Defendants' evidentiary objections as the Court only relies upon paragraphs 5, 8, 9, and 10 of the Declaration of Jenni E. Klose and the attached deposition testimony in reaching its decision. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: COLIN HEILBUT VS. EQUINOX FITNESS SC SAN FRANCISCO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Colin Heilbuts Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc.S Memorandum Of Costs Or, In The Alternative, Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 24, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF COLIN HEILBUT Plaintiff Colin Heilbuts' Motion To Strike Defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc.S Memorandum Of Costs Or, In The Alternative, Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof Plaintiff Colin Heilbut's motion to strike defendant Equinox Holdings, Inc.'s memorandum of costs is DENIED. Plaintiff's alternative motion to tax costs is GRANTED. The parties agree that the costs sought for witness and electronic-document-hosting fees are excessive. Those costs are thus taxed $4,430, reducing the award to $23,228. As explained below, defendant is due those remaining costs. "Except as otherwise expressly provided by statute, a prevailing party is entitled as a matter of right to recover costs in any action or proceeding." (CCP §1032(b).) The Unruh Act provides that only prevailing plaintiffs may obtain attorney fees, saying nothing about costs. (Civ. Code §52(a).) Roman v. BRE Properties, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1040, 1059 holds that defendants may recover Unruh Act costs, because the act lacks cost-shifting language. The cases plaintiff cites are not to the contrary. Turner v. Ass'n. of American Medical Colleges (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 1047, 1059-60 and D.C. v. Harvard-Westlake School (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 836, 864-65 only address attorney fees, not costs. Williams v. Chino Valley Independent Fire District (2015) 61 Cal.4th 97, 115 is a Fair Employment and Housing Act case, and the FEHA expressly denies fees and costs to defendants unless the suit is found frivolous. (Gov't. Code §12965(b).) Plaintiff's financial condition is not a proper consideration in deciding whether a defendant's Unruh Act costs are reasonable. (LAOSD Asbestos Cases (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 1116, 1122-26.) The cases plaintiff cites are under FEHA or regard CCP §998. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER ORAZIO VS. CAMBRIA COMPANY LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Partial Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 24, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANTs JOHN BREKKE, CAMBRIA COMPANY LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Partial Summary Judgment OFF CALENDAR as moot. Defendant filed its motion for summary judgment on the first amended complaint on March 7, 2019. Plaintiff filed the second amended complaint on April 18, 2019, which renders the motion for summary judgment moot. (See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) Pursuant to the court's order of April 11, 2019, defendant may re-new its motion for summary judgment/adjudication to the operative complaint. rulings.Case Title: BAY AREA DIAMOND WHOLESALERS, INC. VS. C.H.A CORP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Demand For Production Of Documents From Def. Cha Corp. And Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,942 Plus Costs Against Defendant Chamdi, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 24, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BAY AREA DIAMOND WHOLESALERS, INC.'s Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Demand For Production Of Documents From Def. Cha Corp. And Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,942 Plus Costs Against Defendant Chamdi, Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied without prejudice as follows. Defendant is technically in compliance with the Request for Production of Documents because it produced all bank statements as they were kept in the course of business. However, Plaintiff may seek further information by interrogatories, depositions or other discovery methods which would require Defendant to specifically identify or produce the relevant checks. The court notes that the bank statements produced indicate in a footnote (designated by a "^") that images of specific checks may be available at chase.com. Thus, Defendant would have the ability in responding to further discovery to identify and to describe and/or produce responsive checks. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
